title: Recording — Browser-Based Audio Capture
problem: The recorder needs to capture multiple songs during a live jam — from a phone browser, one hand, dim room. Between songs the transition to the next recording must be instant. Moments worth keeping must be stampable without interrupting the performance.
analysis: Browser MediaRecorder API with getUserMedia. Typical clip: 5-30 min raw. Audio buffered in memory during recording (30 min AAC ~43MB — fits easily). On stop: upload to Google Drive via resumable upload (server generates upload session URL, browser uploads directly to Drive, bypassing Vercel 4.5MB body limit). Server then transcodes to AAC via FFmpeg (cross-browser compatibility — Chrome outputs WebM, Safari outputs AAC/MP4). Transcoded file replaces the original in Drive. Clip and v1 version rows created in Postgres. Wake Lock API active during recording (screen-on). Level meter via Web Audio API AnalyserNode. Stamps (fire/keep/uncertain/idea) buffered in memory, written to Postgres with clip on stop. 'Next Song' button appears after stop: saves current clip + immediately opens new recording, upload continues in background.
successCriteria:
- Record button triggers microphone permission request on first use
- Level meter visible and responsive during recording (horizontal amber bar, 8px tall, full-width)
- Recording screen is full-screen (tab bar hidden); minimal chrome: timer, level meter, stamps, 'Add note', record/stop button only
- Stamp buttons (fire/keep/uncertain/idea) always visible; one tap stamps current timestamp; haptic feedback on mobile; 72x72px touch targets
- Quick text note button: 'Add note' text link → 1-line bottom sheet, submit adds annotation
- Recording screen usable one-handed in portrait orientation
- Stop → upload begins immediately; 'Next Song' button available without waiting for upload
- Post-record screen shows: clip name (editable inline), duration, stamp count, upload progress
- 'Next Song' opens new recording screen; previous clip uploads in background
- 'Delete recording' option on post-record screen with confirmation sheet: 'Delete this recording? This cannot be undone.'
- During upload: persistent top banner 'Uploading Song N… 47%' with amber progress bar visible on any screen
- Banner dismisses only after upload + transcode confirmed complete
- Wake Lock active during recording (screen stays on)
- iOS warning shown if Wake Lock is unavailable
- Clip appears in session as 'Processing…' until transcode done, then becomes fully interactive
- All clips play back correctly in Chrome and Safari (AAC source)
- Any Editor-role band member can initiate a recording (not just band creator/recorder)
implementation:
1. app/record/page.tsx: full-screen recording screen — timer, level meter, stamp row, Add note link, record/stop button. Tab bar hidden via usePathname().
2. hooks/useRecorder.ts: core hook managing MediaRecorder lifecycle, Web Audio AnalyserNode for level meter, Wake Lock API, stamps buffer, annotations buffer, device enumeration and selection, IndexedDB write on stop (via savePendingUpload), upload start.
3. components/recording/DevicePicker.tsx: bottom sheet listing available audio input devices.
4. app/record/post/page.tsx: post-record screen — clip name (inline edit), duration, stamp count, upload progress bar, Next Song button, Delete recording button.
5. hooks/useUploadProgress.ts: polls /api/clips/[clipId] every 3s until transcodeStatus is DONE or FAILED.
6. lib/pendingUploads.ts: IndexedDB helpers for pending upload persistence.
7. app/api/upload/init/route.ts: generates Drive resumable upload session URL using creator's stored token.
8. app/api/upload/finalize/route.ts: transcodes uploaded audio with FFmpeg, creates Clip + ClipVersion rows in Postgres.
9. STAMP_EMOJI and STAMP_COLORS constants in types/index.ts.

decisions.decision: Resumable Drive upload from browser (bypasses Vercel body limit)
decisions.rationale: Vercel serverless functions have a 4.5MB request body limit. A 30-min recording at 192kbps ~43MB. Sending audio through Vercel would fail. Resumable uploads: server generates a Drive upload session URL, browser POSTs audio blob directly to Drive. Audio never passes through Vercel.
decisions.recommendation: POST /api/upload/init → returns Drive resumable upload URL → browser PUT to Drive URL → POST /api/upload/finalize → server transcodes + creates Postgres records.

decisions.decision: Transcode to AAC immediately on upload
decisions.rationale: MediaRecorder outputs WebM/Opus on Chrome and AAC/MP4 on Safari. WebM does not play in Safari. Transcoding immediately on upload ensures one consistent format (AAC) for all playback, editing, and final render — regardless of which browser the recorder used.
decisions.recommendation: Server-side FFmpeg transcode in /api/upload/finalize. Output: 192kbps AAC. Overwrite original file in Drive.

decisions.decision: Any Editor-role member can record — not just the band creator
decisions.rationale: The upload flow already uses the creator's stored refresh_token on the server to generate the Drive resumable upload session URL. The member's browser uploads directly to the creator's Drive. This means multi-recorder support is not an architectural change — it's a role-permission change. Any Editor can initiate a recording and audio lands in the same Drive folder. Reduces the single-point-of-failure where only one person can capture audio on jam night.
decisions.recommendation: Role check in /api/upload/init: allow role 'editor' or 'recorder' (band creator). Record tab in bottom nav visible to all Editor+ members. Audio always uploaded to creator's Drive via creator's stored token — member's own Drive not involved.

decisions.decision: Two notation types: stamps (typed, no text) and annotations (text notes) — both timestamped
decisions.rationale: Stamps are one-tap typed markers — fire/keep/uncertain/idea — zero friction during playing. Annotations are short text notes for when you want to say something specific. Both appear as pins on the waveform. Keeping them separate preserves the zero-friction stamp UX while allowing richer notes when needed. They serve similar purposes at different levels of effort.
decisions.recommendation: Stamps: one tap picks type (fire red, keep yellow, uncertain orange, idea blue), no text. Annotations: quick text input (bottom sheet), added during recording or in editor. Both in Postgres, both shown as waveform pins.

knownLimitations: iOS Safari stops MediaRecorder if the screen locks or the user switches tabs — Wake Lock API (iOS 16.4+) mitigates accidental sleep; tab switching is user responsibility

If the browser tab is closed before the blob is written to IndexedDB (i.e., during the first few seconds after stop), the audio may be lost — mitigated by writing to IndexedDB immediately on stop before any upload attempt (see feat-recording-resilience)
Resumable upload requires two API round-trips before recording is saved — adds ~2-4s to the stop flow

Any Editor-role member can initiate a recording, but all audio lands in the band creator's Drive — members upload directly to the creator's Drive using the server-generated resumable upload session URL (which uses the creator's stored token)
userGuide: Tap the Record tab from anywhere. On the recording screen, tap the large circle button to start. The timer runs; the level meter shows audio input. Tap stamp buttons (fire/keep/uncertain/idea) any time to mark notable moments. Tap 'Add note' to type a quick text annotation. Tap the square stop button to finish. After stopping, name the clip on the post-record screen, tap 'Next Song' to immediately start another recording while the previous one uploads in the background, or navigate away.
